title: 3 Legal Considerations
body_text:
3	Legal Considerations
3.1	Overview of Legal Considerations
Financial institutions are responsible for complying with applicable legislation and regulations. The information provided in this Chapter contains a summary of the main statutes and regulations that pertain to this Part and is provided for convenience only. It doesnât take the place of statutes and regulations. We recommend that you refer to the actual statute or regulation for the full text.
3.2	CPA Rules
Payments Canada develops and implements the rules that apply to the clearing and settlement of different types of payments between its member financial institutions. Refer to the Payments Canada website for more information on CPA Rules.
CPA Rule A3, Endorsement and Identification of Paper Items
â Outlines procedures for the endorsement and identification of paper payment items.
CPA Standard 006, Specifications for MICR-Encoded Payment Items
â Defines the location and background design of the necessary data elements for imageable MICR-encoded payment items and other MICR-encoded payment items.
3.3	Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act and Regulations
The
Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act
(
PCMLTFA
)
and
PCMLTF Regulations
apply to financial institutions and their agents (e.g., deposit-taking agents). Contact your AML Compliance Officer or FINTRAC for more information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
END
